column1: # Per-cell scalar values are STRICTLY from {0.0
#   0.7 = HIGH  (direct forge of probe's signal OR probe is designed to catch this exact mitigation)
# A reduction value of 0.7 means the spoofer mitigation closes 70% of the probe's detection gap.
# A detection value of 0.7 means the detector probe has 70% confidence of catching this mitigation's
# spoof artifact at the moment of application. Reductions and detections are NOT constrained to sum.
# 1. Read ml-adversary-models.md §6 Layer x Adversary table to identify which LAYERS each mitigation
#    a. If probe.layer is the primary target of mitigation.layer:
#       - reduction = HIGH (0.7) if mitigation directly forges that probe's signal
#       - reduction = MED  (0.4) if mitigation indirectly hides the signal via parent-layer
#       - reduction = LOW  (0.1) if mitigation creates cross-probe coherence support only
#    b. If probe.layer is orthogonal to mitigation.layer: reduction = 0.0
# 3. Detection prior is the probe's expected catch-rate against THIS mitigation's spoof artifact:
#    - HIGH (0.7) if the probe is designed to detect this exact mitigation type
#    - MED  (0.4) if the probe is sensitive to the parent layer
#    - LOW  (0.1) if the probe is collateral-sensitive
